entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: The crop used in the experiment to evaluate phosphorus use efficiency.

entities.name: VESICULAR ARBUSCULAR MYCORRHIZAL FUNGI
entities.description: Fungi cultures used for co-inoculation to improve nutrient uptake in maize.

entities.name: PHOSPHATE-SOLUBILIZING BACTERIA
entities.description: Bacteria cultures used for co-inoculation to solubilize phosphorus for plant uptake.

entities.name: PSEUDOMONAS STRIATA
entities.description: A specific species of phosphate-solubilizing bacteria used in the study.

entities.name: GLOMUS INTRARADICES
entities.description: A specific species of vesicular arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi (VAMT) used in the study.

entities.name: GLOMUS MOSSEAE
entities.description: A specific species of vesicular arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi (VAMI) used in the study.

entities.name: ACIDIC ALFISOL
entities.description: A phosphorus-deficient acidic soil type used as the growth medium.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: The essential nutrient whose use and availability were being improved.

entities.name: P2O5 DOSE
entities.description: The amount of phosphorus fertilizer applied based on recommended doses.

entities.name: SOIL-TEST CROP RESPONSE MODEL
entities.description: The model used to determine the recommended dose of P2O5.

entities.name: GLASSHOUSE POT EXPERIMENT
entities.description: The experimental setup used to conduct the study.

entities.name: MYCORRHIZAL ROOT COLONIZATION
entities.description: The extent of fungal colonization within the roots of the maize plant.

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The overall yield and growth performance of the maize crop.

entities.name: GRAIN PROTEIN CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of protein found in the maize grains.

entities.name: NUTRIENT UPTAKE
entities.description: The amount of nutrients absorbed by the maize plant from the soil.

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY STATUS
entities.description: The nutrient levels and health of the soil, specifically regarding phosphorus.
